header.title: Visitor FAQs
header.lead: Thinking about visiting? Here's what to expect. However you come, you'll be welcome.

questions.a: We are the Unitarian Universalist Society in Wellesley Hills, a liberal religious congregation established in 1871. Our only creed is love.
A welcoming faith tradition that draws wisdom from many sources and encourages each person to follow their own spiritual path, guided by shared values rather than a required doctrine.
Sunday services last about an hour and include music, readings, a sermon, time for reflection, and song. Come as you are — there's no dress code.
We gather for fellowship and refreshments, in person and on Zoom. It's a great time to meet people and ask questions.

questions.q: When and where do you meet?
questions.a: Sundays at 10:30am (September–mid-June) at 309 Washington Street, Wellesley Hills. Summer services are lay-led and meet in the chapel.
Yes. Services and fellowship hour are available remotely — look for the livestream and Zoom links on our homepage.
Yes, our campus is wheelchair accessible. Contact the office with any specific needs and we'll be glad to help.
Parking is available on-site and on nearby streets. Arrive a few minutes early and a greeter will help you find your way.
Absolutely. We offer religious education and welcome children in worship. Wiggles and noise are part of community life.

questions.q: What about support during hard times?
questions.a: Our Caring & Companioning Team offers support to members and friends facing illness, loss, or other challenges.
